title: ETS ownership transfer — heir and give_away
purpose: By default, an ETS table dies with its owner. The `{heir, Pid, Data}` option designates a successor — when the owner dies, the table is transferred to the heir instead of being deleted. `ets:give_away/3` transfers ownership explicitly. The heir receives an `{'ETS-TRANSFER', Tab, FromPid, Data}` message. OTP supervisors use this to preserve tables across process restarts.
task: Implement the heir option on ets:new, the ets:give_away/3 BIF, and the ETS-TRANSFER message delivery.

requirements.title: Heir option on ets:new
acceptance: `{heir, Pid, HeirData}` parsed from ets:new options, stored in metadata
HeirData is an arbitrary term stored alongside the heir PID
Invalid heir PID (non-existent process) is accepted at creation (checked at transfer time)
Test: create table with heir option, verify metadata contains heir info

requirements.title: Owner death transfers to heir
requirements.spec: Modify the owner-death cleanup (B-090 R4) to check for an heir before deleting.
acceptance: When owner dies and heir is set: transfer ownership to heir, deliver ETS-TRANSFER message
When owner dies and heir is dead/nonexistent: delete the table (fallback)
When owner dies and heir is None: delete the table (existing behavior)
ETS-TRANSFER message format: `{'ETS-TRANSFER', TableId, FromPid, HeirData}` delivered to heir's mailbox
After transfer, the table's owner field is updated to the heir's PID
Test: owner dies, heir receives ETS-TRANSFER, table is accessible by heir

requirements.title: ets:give_away/3
acceptance: `ets:give_away(Tab, Pid, GiftData)` transfers ownership from caller to Pid
Only the current owner can give away
Recipient receives `{'ETS-TRANSFER', Tab, FromPid, GiftData}` message
After give_away, caller can no longer write to a protected/private table
Test: give_away to another process, verify ownership transfer and message delivery

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement recursive heir chains (heir of heir) — if the heir is also dead, table is deleted
SHALL NOT implement ets:setopts for changing heir after creation — heir is set at creation or via give_away
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
